title: caltha palustris var. alba
intro: Caltha palustris var. alba, also known as marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Caltha palustris var. alba is typically made into a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can be used externally to soothe skin conditions or internally to promote overall well-being.
